questions.question: What does @WebMvcTest(UserController.class) start?
options: The full Spring Boot application context including database and services
Only Spring MVC — web layer, Jackson, MockMvc — without JPA or real service beans
Only the UserController class without any Spring infrastructure
A real embedded Tomcat server on a random port

questions.question: What is the purpose of @MockBean in a @WebMvcTest?
options: It creates a real bean but wraps it in a spy that records calls
It replaces the real service bean with a Mockito mock in the Spring test context
It prevents the bean from being created to speed up the test
It marks the bean as optional so the test doesn't fail if it's missing

questions.question: Which test annotation starts an H2 in-memory database and rolls back after each test?
options: @SpringBootTest with H2 on the classpath
@DataJpaTest
@WebMvcTest with @Transactional
@AutoConfigureTestDatabase

questions.question: Why is a unit test for UserService that uses new UserService(mockRepo, mockEmail) faster than a @SpringBootTest?
options: JUnit runs faster when Spring annotations are absent
No Spring context is created — no classpath scanning, no bean initialization, no server startup
Mockito mocks run on native code while Spring beans use reflection
Unit tests skip the JVM warm-up phase

questions.question: MockMvc.perform(get("/api/users/1")) does what?
options: Makes a real HTTP request to a running server
Simulates an HTTP request through the Spring MVC processing pipeline without a real network call
Calls the controller method directly, bypassing all filters
Creates a mock HTTP client that records requests for later verification
